name: skDecode
label: Algorithm 25
body_raw: (𝜌, 𝐾, 𝑡𝑟, 𝑦0, … , 𝑦ℓ−1, 𝑧0, … , 𝑧𝑘−1, 𝑤0, … , 𝑤𝑘−1) ∈ 𝔹32 × 𝔹32 × 𝔹64 × (𝔹32⋅bitlen (2𝜂)) ℓ × (𝔹32⋅bitlen (2𝜂)) 𝑘 × (𝔹32𝑑) 𝑘 ← 𝑠𝑘
for 𝑖 from 0 to ℓ − 1 do
𝐬1[𝑖] ← BitUnpack(𝑦𝑖, 𝜂, 𝜂) ▷ this may lie outside [−𝜂, 𝜂] if input is malformed
for 𝑖 from 0 to 𝑘 − 1 do
𝐬2[𝑖] ← BitUnpack(𝑧𝑖, 𝜂, 𝜂) ▷ this may lie outside [−𝜂, 𝜂] if input is malformed
for 𝑖 from 0 to 𝑘 − 1 do
𝐭0[𝑖] ← BitUnpack(𝑤𝑖, 2𝑑−1 − 1, 2𝑑−1) ▷ this is always in the correct range

return (𝜌, 𝐾, 𝑡𝑟, 𝐬1, 𝐬2, 𝐭0) Next, sigEncode and sigDecode translate ML-DSA signatures into byte strings and vice versa. When verifying a signature, sigDecode might take input that comes from an untrusted source. Thus, care is required when using BitUnpack. As used here, BitUnpack always returns values in the correct range.
